name: the-dictators-handbook
description: Bruce Bueno de Mesquita and Alastair Smith's The Dictator's Handbook — game theory toolkit revealing why bad behavior is almost always good politics, how leaders stay in power, and what drives political decision-making
useCase: Understand selectorate theory, predict political behavior, analyze institutional incentives, and design better governance systems
knownRisks: The theory's reduction of all political motivation to survival is controversial — some leaders may be driven by ideology
The game-theoretic framework can feel overly cynical to readers who believe in public service
Historical examples are selectively chosen to support the theory — contrary cases may be underemphasized
